design_philosophy.core_loop: Maintain power as El Presidente through balancing faction satisfaction, economic management, and Cold War superpower relations while issuing governmental edicts
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Player must juggle competing demands from multiple factions (military, rebels, superpowers, populace) while staying in power; any single dissatisfied group can overthrow the regime
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rule a Caribbean banana republic as a satirized dictator, with freedom to be either authoritarian or democratic, corrupt or benevolent
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game is deliberately tongue-in-cheek about authoritarianism and Cold War geopolitics; presents semi-democratic banana republics with dark humor while tackling topics like electoral fraud, totalitarianism, and corporate/superpower intervention. Uses real historical figures (Che Guevara, Fidel Castro, Pinochet) alongside fictional leaders for satirical effect

difficulty.continues_system: No continues system; regime overthrow ends the game
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extensive player agency: can customize nearly all starting conditions including political difficulty, economic difficulty, map size/steepness, game duration (10-70 years), victory conditions, and El Presidente personality traits that affect faction attitudes

progression.upgrade_system: No traditional upgrade system; progression through unlocking new edicts and buildings based on game state and construction
progression.unlock_mechanism: New edicts and governmental actions unlock based on available buildings and funds; player can research/construct new building types to access new capabilities
replayability_hooks: Multiple predefined scenarios with varying difficulty levels
Custom game mode with fully configurable starting conditions
Different El Presidente personas (real historical figures and fictional characters) with unique personality traits affecting gameplay
Variable victory conditions and game duration (10-70 year spans covering 1950-2000)
Random map generation with customizable island parameters

world_building_techniques: Cold War era setting with historical context (1950-2000 timespan)
Satirical tone toward semi-democratic banana republics and dictatorships
References to real historical interventions (United Fruit Company implied, US/Soviet involvement)
Cultural soundtrack with Latin-styled Caribbean music (award-winning original composition)

faction_indicators: Rebel faction approval (can storm Presidential palace if unhappy)
Soviet Union approval (supplies foreign aid, can launch invasion)
United States approval (supplies foreign aid, can launch invasion)
General populace approval (can vote leader out of office in elections)
Individual faction and power group satisfaction metrics
danger_signals: Military dissatisfaction leading to coup risk
Low populace approval threatening electoral defeat
Foreign military bases demanding specific tasks under threat of overthrow
environmental_storytelling: Building construction patterns reflect economic focus and faction appeasement
Island customization choices affect starting political/economic conditions
Swiss bank account accumulation reflects personal corruption level
Superpower military base presence indicates geopolitical alignment during Cold War
